skill.name: image-outpainting
skill.description: Use the RunComfy CLI to outpaint still images, extend canvas edges, and convert aspect ratios while preserving the original subject.
skill.summary: Outpaint images through RunComfy models with route guidance for aspect ratio changes, uncropping, and reference-based extensions.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell, network, filesystem, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. AI review found no evidence of malicious intent, prompt injection, weak cryptography, or system reconnaissance; the confirmed risks are expected RunComfy CLI execution, outbound API use, and local token storage.

medium_findings.title: External RunComfy CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill allows Bash(runcomfy *) and provides runcomfy run examples. This is core functionality, but it executes an external CLI and can submit prompts and image URLs to RunComfy services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and command examples directly confirm external command usage. The scope is limited to runcomfy commands, so the risk is moderate rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Outbound API Use With User Images
medium_findings.description: The skill states that the CLI posts to the Model API, polls status, and downloads results. User prompts and image URLs are intended to leave the local environment for RunComfy processing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly describes outbound API behavior and an endpoint allowlist. This is expected for the skill, but users should understand the data transfer.

medium_findings.title: Local API Token Storage
medium_findings.description: The skill documents that runcomfy login writes an API token to a hidden home directory path. The file mode is described as 0600, but local credential storage remains a user-visible risk factor.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and permissions are stated directly. I found no instruction to read or exfiltrate the token, so this is a credential-handling concern, not confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded RunComfy URLs Are Expected Endpoints
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to RunComfy home, model, workflow, documentation, and related skill pages. They match the declared service purpose and do not show unrelated exfiltration destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains are visible and consistent with the RunComfy skill purpose. No evidence found of unrelated network targets or covert telemetry.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Not Supported
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain description text, model guidance, prompts, workflow names, or exit-code documentation. No cryptographic operation or weak algorithm use was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose and sample prompts at the cited locations, not crypto code. The file is a Markdown skill document with no implemented cryptographic routines.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Not Supported
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss model selection and multi-step outpainting. No commands inspect the host, enumerate files, collect environment data, or probe system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible workflow guidance. No evidence found of reconnaissance commands or intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Access Scoped To runcomfy
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access for runcomfy commands. This is narrowly scoped but still permits external process execution through the agent tool layer.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools header is direct evidence. Scope is constrained to runcomfy, which reduces but does not remove execution risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Model API Submission
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill describes posting requests to the RunComfy Model API and downloading results. User image URLs and prompts can be transmitted to the provider.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicitly documented in the skill. It is legitimate for image outpainting but requires user awareness.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential File In Hidden Home Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents token storage under ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json. Hidden credential files should be protected and excluded from logs or shared archives.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact token path and file mode are stated. No evidence found that the skill reads the token directly outside normal CLI authentication.

content.user_title: Extend Images Beyond Their Original Frame
content.value_statement: Cropped images often do not fit new layouts or channels. This skill routes outpainting tasks through RunComfy models to extend the canvas while preserving the original subject.
actual_capabilities: Routes outpainting requests to suitable RunComfy image edit endpoints.
Provides CLI command patterns for extending image canvas edges.
Supports aspect ratio changes such as square to widescreen or portrait to vertical.
Recommends model choices for prompt-based, reference-based, and brand-style outpainting.
Includes prompt guidance for preserving the visible subject and matching surrounding context.
Documents retry-relevant exit codes and related RunComfy resources.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and a valid RunComfy login or token.
Sends prompts and image URLs to RunComfy services for processing.
Does not provide mask-driven local inpainting inside the existing canvas.
Dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows must be opened outside the CLI.

use_cases.title: Create Campaign Banners
use_cases.description: Extend a product or portrait image into a 16:9 or 21:9 banner while keeping the main subject stable.

use_cases.title: Adapt Social Media Assets
use_cases.description: Convert square images into vertical or widescreen formats for platform-specific posts and reels.

use_cases.title: Repair Cropped Reference Images
use_cases.description: Uncrop portraits, product photos, or archival images with prompts that describe missing surrounding context.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Canvas Extension
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner outpaint request for one image
prompt_templates.prompt: Outpaint this image to 16:9. Extend the left and right edges with matching background, lighting, and camera distance. Keep the original subject unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Social Format Conversion
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a vertical image from a square source
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the image-outpainting skill to convert this square image to 9:16 for social video. Extend the top and bottom naturally, and preserve the current subject.

prompt_templates.title: Reference-Guided Extension
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a second image to guide style or environment
prompt_templates.prompt: Outpaint the main image into a wide hero frame. Use the reference image only for surrounding style, texture, and lighting. Do not replace the main subject.

prompt_templates.title: Model Route Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: Choose the best RunComfy route for this outpaint. Explain whether Nano Banana 2 Edit, GPT Image 2 Edit, or FLUX Kontext Pro fits best, then run it with preservation-focused wording.

output_examples.input: A square product photo needs a wide ecommerce banner version.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects a RunComfy image edit route, asks for the source image URL if missing, and prepares an outpaint prompt that extends side context while preserving the product.

output_examples.input: A head-and-shoulders portrait should reveal more of the upper body.
output_examples.output: The assistant uses a downward uncrop prompt, keeps facial features unchanged, and saves the generated result in the configured output directory.

output_examples.input: A historical photo needs missing background extended using reference images.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends the reference-capable route and frames the prompt around period-appropriate lighting, texture, and preservation of the original visible area.

best_practices: Use image URLs that the user explicitly provided for the current task.
State the target aspect ratio and extension direction at the start of the prompt.
Include preservation instructions for faces, products, logos, and other important visible areas.
anti_patterns: Do not use arbitrary remote install scripts for the RunComfy CLI.
Do not submit private images or confidential references without user approval.
Do not expect perfect seam repair from one pass when the source image has complex boundaries.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an agent use RunComfy models to extend the canvas around an existing still image.

faq.question: Does it generate images locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the RunComfy CLI, which sends requests to RunComfy services.

faq.question: Can it change a square image to widescreen?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for changing aspect ratios such as 1:1 to 16:9 or 21:9.

faq.question: Does it support reference images?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends GPT Image 2 Edit when the outpaint should match a reference style or layout.

faq.question: Where are generated files saved?
faq.answer: The examples use the RunComfy CLI output directory option, commonly shown as ./out.

faq.question: What data leaves my environment?
faq.answer: Prompts, image URLs, and related request data may be sent to RunComfy for model processing.
